channel.title: SteadyDevs Blog - Legacy .NET Systems Insights
channel.description: Expert insights on maintaining, stabilizing, and improving legacy .NET systems. Practical tips and real-world case studies from 11+ years of production experience.

item.title: E-Invoice for B2C: How Retailers Handle Walk-In Customers Without Issuing Thousands of Invoices
item.description: Most walk-in customers don't ask for an e-invoice—so do you really have to issue one for every sale? Here's how consolidated e-invoicing works for B2C retail, what your POS needs to support, and the mistakes that trigger compliance problems.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: MyInvois API Integration: What Your Developer Needs to Know Before Connecting Your Legacy System
item.description: Connecting a legacy .NET system to LHDN's MyInvois API is more than a single endpoint call. Here's what actually goes into integration—authentication, digital signing, validation, and the pitfalls that catch most developers off guard.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: What a Proper System Handover Document Should Contain (Free Checklist)
item.description: When a developer leaves, the difference between a smooth transition and a crisis is one thing: documentation. Here's exactly what a proper handover document should contain—a practical checklist you can hand to any developer before they walk out the door.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: Windows Server & SQL Server End-of-Life: Is Your Legacy System Running on Borrowed Time?
item.description: Your business system works, so the aging Windows Server and SQL Server underneath it are easy to ignore. But when Microsoft ends support, those quiet servers become a security, compliance, and insurance liability overnight. Here's how to find out where you stand and what to do about it.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: Why Your POS/ERP Vendor Won't Support Your Customizations (And What to Do About It)
item.description: You paid for a POS or ERP system, added essential customizations, and now the vendor refuses support. This is a common trap for Malaysian SMEs. Here's why it happens, how to avoid it, and what to do if you're already stuck.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: LHDN E-Invoice Phase 3, 4 & 5: What Small Businesses Must Do Before the Deadline (Mid-2026 Update)
item.description: The e-invoice net has reached small businesses. If your annual turnover is between RM500,000 and RM25 million, your mandatory date has either passed or is about to. Here's the current rollout schedule, what the grace period really means, and exactly what to do now.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: The Small Business Guide to System Integration: Connecting Your POS, Accounting, and Inventory
item.description: Tired of manually transferring data between your POS, accounting software, and inventory system? Learn how system integration works, what's actually involved, and realistic costs—based on real integration projects for Malaysian SMEs.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: How to Add Modern Features to a 10-Year-Old System (Without Breaking Everything)
item.description: Your legacy system works, but customers want mobile apps, API integrations, and real-time features. Here's how to modernize incrementally without the risk of a catastrophic rewrite—with real examples from production systems.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: Is Your Legacy System PDPA-Compliant? A Practical Guide for Malaysian Businesses
item.description: The Personal Data Protection Act isn't optional—and your legacy system probably wasn't built with PDPA in mind. Here's how to audit your system, identify gaps, and implement compliance without a costly rebuild.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: 5 Questions to Ask Before Hiring a Developer to Fix Your Legacy System
item.description: Not all developers can handle legacy systems. Hiring the wrong one wastes money and makes problems worse. Here are 5 critical questions that separate capable legacy system specialists from developers who will fail.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: The True Cost of 'Free' Technical Support from Your Developer
item.description: Your developer says "just call me when something breaks" with no formal contract. Sounds convenient and cost-effective—until you calculate what this informal arrangement actually costs your business in hidden expenses and risk.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: The RM50,000 Question: Cloud Migration vs. On-Premise Modernization for Legacy .NET
item.description: Should you migrate your legacy .NET system to Azure cloud or modernize on-premise? Both paths cost RM40,000-80,000+ but solve different problems. Here's a practical decision framework based on real Malaysian SME scenarios.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: Should You Add Features to a Failing System? (The Stabilize-First Framework)
item.description: Your legacy system is unstable, but business needs new features. Adding features to fragile systems usually backfires. Here's how to decide when to stabilize first vs. when feature work makes sense—and what the trade-offs actually look like.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: Your Developer Left. Now What? (Emergency Recovery Guide)
item.description: Your only developer quit, disappeared, or can't be reached. Your legacy system is running but nobody knows how it works. Here's your immediate action plan for recovering from developer departure without destroying the business.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: E-Invoice Mandate: What Happened to Businesses That Missed the Deadline
item.description: We're now deep into Malaysia's e-invoice rollout. Some businesses met their deadlines, others didn't. Here's what actually happened to the ones who missed it—the real consequences, recovery paths, and lessons learned.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: The Excel Trap: When Spreadsheets Cost More Than Proper Software
item.description: Excel is free and flexible—until it's not. Many Malaysian SMEs running RM5M-20M operations still rely on spreadsheets for critical business functions. Here's when Excel stops being cost-effective and starts being a liability.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: Your Developer Says the System is 'Too Old to Fix': Is That Actually True?
item.description: When a developer tells you your legacy system can't be fixed, are they right—or just reluctant? Here's how to verify the diagnosis, spot red flags of lazy assessment, and get a real answer about your system's future.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: The 3-Month System Stabilization Sprint: A Framework for Crisis Mode
item.description: When your legacy system is actively causing business problems, you need a structured approach to stabilize fast. Here's a proven 90-day framework for taking systems from crisis to manageable—without a complete rewrite.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: From Manual to Automated: What to Expect from Invoice Automation
item.description: Three Malaysian business scenarios that show what happens when you automate invoicing—measurable time savings, error elimination, cash flow improvement, and unexpected benefits most businesses don't anticipate.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: What Happens to Your Business When the Developer Who Knows Your System Leaves
item.description: Most businesses don't realise how much critical knowledge lives in one person's head — until they're gone. Here's what actually happens, and how to protect yourself before it becomes a crisis.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: Why Your POS System Keeps Getting Slower (And What's Actually Happening Inside)
item.description: POS slowness is rarely a hardware problem. It's almost always something that's been quietly building inside the system for months. Here's what's actually happening — and why throwing more server power at it usually doesn't help.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: 5 Signs Your Business Is Ready for Automation (And What to Automate First)
item.description: Business automation isn't about replacing people — it's about stopping them from doing work that software should be doing. Here are 5 clear signs your business is ready, and what to automate first.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: The Hidden Cost of Manual Invoicing in Malaysian SMEs
item.description: Manual invoicing costs Malaysian SMEs more than you think. Discover the hidden expenses in time, errors, cash flow delays, and missed opportunities—and why automation is no longer optional.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: How to Know If Your Legacy System Can Be Saved (Honest Assessment)
item.description: Not every legacy system is worth saving. Here's a practical framework to assess whether yours can be fixed, should be replaced, or needs a hybrid approach—based on 11 years of real-world stabilization projects.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: The Price of Ignoring That 'Small' Bug in Your Production System
item.description: That minor bug you're postponing? It's costing you more than you think. Real examples of how small ignored bugs cascade into expensive problems—and why fixing them now is always cheaper than later.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: What Happens When You Delay Legacy System Maintenance
item.description: Every month you delay maintaining your legacy system, the problems compound. Here's the predictable cascade of consequences—and why fixing problems early costs a fraction of waiting until they're critical.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: E-Invoice Malaysia: Is Your Legacy System Ready?
item.description: Malaysia's e-invoice mandate is already rolling out. Large and medium businesses are already mandated. If your legacy system can't adapt, you're facing compliance crisis. Here's what you need to know and do now.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: AI-Assisted Development Without the Code Chaos: Building a Pet Booking System
item.description: Can AI tools like Claude actually speed up development without creating unmaintainable code? Here's what happened when I used AI to build a complex booking system—and how to avoid the common pitfalls.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: When to Refactor vs. Rewrite Your Legacy .NET System: A Practical Guide
item.description: Facing a difficult legacy .NET system? Learn when refactoring makes sense versus when a full rewrite is justified, based on 11 years of real-world experience with production systems.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs

item.title: 5 Warning Signs Your Legacy .NET System Needs Professional Attention
item.description: Is your legacy .NET system showing signs of trouble? Learn the 5 critical warning signs that indicate it's time to seek professional help before small issues become major problems.
item.dc:creator: SteadyDevs
